rationale: Glutamate racemase uses cysteine acid-base racemization chemistry, which is not represented by the current seed fingerprints.
Class A beta-lactamase is a serine beta-lactamase but not the Ser-His-Asp/Glu triad hydrolase fingerprint currently defined.
3-hydroxydecanoyl-ACP dehydratase performs dehydration chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Deoxyribonuclease IV is a nuclease hydrolase; nuclease catalysis commonly depends on metal-activated water.
Ornithine carbamoyltransferase performs carbamoyl transfer chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Amine dehydrogenase redox chemistry is not represented by the current seed fingerprints.
Vanadate-dependent chloride peroxidase is peroxidase chemistry but not the heme-dependent peroxidase or oxidase seed fingerprint.
Class B1 beta-lactamase is a metallo-beta-lactamase using metal-activated hydrolysis.
Class B1 beta-lactamase is a metallo-beta-lactamase using metal-activated hydrolysis.
Purine nucleoside phosphorylase performs phosphorolysis rather than the current hydrolase seed mechanisms.
Glycine amidinotransferase performs group transfer chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Alpha-mannosidase is a glycosidase but its acid-base glycoside hydrolysis mechanism is not represented by the current seed fingerprints.
Fumarate reductase is redox chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Malate dehydrogenase and decarboxylating chemistry is outside the current seed fingerprints.
Aralkylamine N-acetyltransferase performs acetyl transfer chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Glycine N-methyltransferase is a methyltransferase outside the current seed fingerprints.
4-chlorobenzoyl-CoA dehalogenase has covalent dehalogenase chemistry not represented by the current seed fingerprints.
N-carbamoylsarcosine amidase is not confidently mapped to a current seed fingerprint without a more specific amidase fingerprint.
Phosphatidylinositol-specific phospholipase C chemistry is not represented by the current seed fingerprints.
Phospholipase C hydrolyzes phosphodiester bonds and fits a metal-dependent hydrolase seed mechanism in this coarse label set.
Phosphoinositide phospholipase C hydrolyzes a phosphodiester with metal-stabilized water chemistry, fitting the coarse metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Glutaminase-asparaginase uses N-terminal threonine amidohydrolase chemistry not represented by the current seed fingerprints.
NAD(P)H dehydrogenase quinone chemistry is flavin redox hydride transfer, not the current flavin monooxygenase seed mechanism.
Formate C-acetyltransferase uses glycyl radical transfer chemistry outside the current radical SAM seed fingerprint.
Thymidylate synthase uses folate-dependent methyl transfer and reductive chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Lactoylglutathione lyase uses metal-assisted lyase/isomerization chemistry rather than metal-activated hydrolysis.
Methylmalonyl-CoA epimerase uses metal-associated acid-base epimerization rather than the current hydrolase seed mechanisms.
Catechol 2,3-dioxygenase is a non-heme iron dioxygenase and does not match the current flavin or heme oxygenase seeds.
Phosphorylase kinase performs ATP-dependent phosphoryl transfer outside the current seed fingerprints.
S-2-haloacid dehalogenase uses covalent aspartate dehalogenase chemistry not represented by the current seed fingerprints.
GTP cyclohydrolase I uses zinc-assisted water attack in a hydrolytic ring-opening step, fitting the coarse metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Copper nitrite reductase performs metal-centered electron-transfer reduction chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Deoxyribonuclease I hydrolyzes phosphodiester bonds with metal-assisted water attack, fitting the coarse metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Purple acid phosphatase performs metal-assisted phosphate ester hydrolysis, fitting the metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Alkaline phosphatase uses metal-supported phosphate ester hydrolysis and is closest to the coarse metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Cyclomaltodextrin glucanotransferase uses glycosyl transfer and glycoside chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
